claim: The explorer's claim about a broken import path is contradicted by actual file inspection; src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts has no import from ../adapters/agent-contract.js

evidence.summary: Lines 1-12 show all imports in the file; there is no import from ../adapters/agent-contract.js or any use of normalizeAgentProvider
evidence.why: Direct inspection of the actual file content contradicts the explorer's claim about line 4

evidence.summary: Line 4 imports from 'node:path' (dirname, isAbsolute, join, relative, resolve), not from ../adapters/agent-contract.js
evidence.why: This directly contradicts the explorer's assertion that line 4 contains a broken import

evidence.summary: The complete file is 617 lines and contains no reference to normalizeAgentProvider or imports from an adapters directory
evidence.why: Full file read confirms no such import exists anywhere in the file

evidence.summary: The src/adapters/ directory does not exist; ls returns 'No such file or directory'
evidence.why: This confirms that even if there were an import from ../adapters/, the target directory would not exist

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function exists in src/runtime/adapters/agent-contract.ts but is not imported by evidence-dispatch.ts
evidence.why: While the function exists in the correct location, it is not used or imported in the file the explorer claimed was broken

evidence.summary: Test imports buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt, buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan, and runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime from evidence-dispatch.js
evidence.why: If the import were broken as claimed, these test imports would fail at module load time

assumptions: The explorer may have been examining an older version of the file before it was fixed or modified
The git status shows src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts as modified (M), indicating recent changes
Previous evidence results from dispatch-20260601033747536-4h57gt may reflect a different file state
risks: Accepting the explorer's false claim as fact would incorrectly block the demand dispatch based on a non-existent issue
The explorer's high confidence rating on a false claim suggests they may have examined a stale or cached version of the file
If the import was recently fixed, the actual runtime behavior may differ from both the explorer's claim and the current file state
missing: No evidence of when the import was changed from the explorer's claimed broken state to the current working state
No verification that the current file has actually been tested at runtime (import success does not guarantee runtime correctness)
No evidence that provider isolation logic has been tested in live execution with real provider binaries

result.notes: Critical contradiction detected. The explorer claimed line 4 of src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts imports from ../adapters/agent-contract.js (a broken path), but direct file inspection shows line 4 imports from 'node:path'. The complete file has 617 lines and contains no import of normalizeAgentProvider or any reference to an adapters directory. The src/adapters/ directory does not exist. While normalizeAgentProvider exists in src/runtime/adapters/agent-contract.ts, it is not imported by evidence-dispatch.ts. The explorer's evidence cannot be reconciled with the actual file state. This may indicate file changes since the explorer's examination or stale evidence from a previous dispatch run.
